blocks.text: We have all gotten a sunburn once or twice in our lives, but how many have gotten a starburn?
The goal of this project is to collect enough photons from a distant star to give someone a (very small), but noticeable burn on a part of their body of their choosing.
Some envelope math suggests this could be done with a small array (~12) of 1m collectors with some clever fiber coupling, or something like a 10ft mosaic with some active optical controls.
It could be a very “fast” version of this sort of design. The only additional work would be adding a microscope objective to do the final reduction to a small enough spot to cause a small injury
The participant can also be given a small amount of ink to rub into the burn to make it a full scar. A permanent mark from a far away world.
Many options to make this cheaper, but less pure:

items.text: It would be trivial to do from the moon, this is still reflected sunlight after all
Another option is using the starlight to prime a laser cavity (amplification of the same photon… medium purity)

Another option is using the starlight to charge a battery/circuit, then doing a discharge to get the burn. Lower purity, but would work with even a very small scope. Maybe this is an “idea” level demo you build to fundraise the larger one? IDK…
